text:
Once upon a time in the tranquil village of Willowbrook, there lived a quiet girl named Lily. She loved collecting wildflowers and weaving tiny wreaths for her friends' birthdays. One day, during a late winter bloom, Lily discovered a forgotten journal in an old oak tree. As she browsed its contents, she learned of a mystical bird who could grant three wishes every decade.

The first wish she made was to understand the stars. Night after night, she stargared, weaving tales of astrology and distant worlds into her dreams. Soon, she could predict eclipses and plan weddings.

Her second wish was for a garden that glowed like stars. She planted twinkling flowers that sang melodies in secret languages. Her garden flourished, enchanting everyone who visited.

The third wish was for peace. Lily petitioned for a day where no war ever touched the land again. She helped organize workshops on diplomacy and harmony.
